parsed_review.summary: CP00-387 is a clean, descriptor-only RP12.P08 workflow/permission/fixture closeout slice (40 units, risk B, RP12.P08.M04.S13-RP12.P08.M06.S08). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly NOT asserted: manifest.json reports production_ready=false, status=implemented_pending_review, runtime_ready=false, and command-evidence.json reports production_ready=false with gate_outcome=implementation_gates_passed_post_review_closeout_pending. The descriptor-verification flag (billing_core_p08_workflow_permission_slice_descriptor_verified) is fully supported: (1) all 7 required exports exist and follow the established CP386 pattern (service.js:2974-3045, validators.js:4384-4543); (2) freezeCp387Result (service.js:2926-2968) stamps comprehensive no-write/no-real-data guards (runtime dispatch, DB/object-storage writes, permission/audit evaluation, hermes runtime receipt, real fixture loading all false; synthetic_only/no_real_data true); (3) tests model.test.js:1589-1639 assert coverage, descriptor-only rows, and authority boundaries; (4) validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs deep-equals contract sections against registry constants; (5) command-evidence.json records all required validation commands at exit 0. Internal consistency holds across registry binding, contract (cp387_requirements / cp387_no_write_attestation / current_pack / descriptor projection), manifest, next-pack-queue, and closeout-pack-plan — all agree on counts, range, distributions, upstream CP00-386, and handoff to CP00-388 / RP12.P08.M06.S09. Authority boundary is preserved: Claude is read-only and not final approver (claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, allowed tools = Read/Grep/Glob), enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation, and human final approval remains required. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt descriptors (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false). Review-hardening posture is sound: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1238-byte stdout), no 0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output in evidence, forbidden_review_evidence list enforced, and no pre-filled claude-review-result.json exists (only manifest.json + command-evidence.json present, with post-review artifacts correctly pending). No P0/P1/P2 issues found. One non-blocking P3 advisory: changed-file-scope.json double-categorizes two untracked paths.

findings.title: changed-file-scope.json double-categorizes two untracked paths (untracked_implementation_files and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved)
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-387/ are listed in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 131-132) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 137-138). new-session-handoff.md is genuinely unrelated to this pack (it is not in manifest.json source_refs/evidence_refs, which authoritatively scope only the 8 billing implementation files plus the cp00-387 evidence dir), while cp00-387/ IS pack-relevant. The dual listing is a benign heuristic artifact in the scope-tracking file and does not cause any unrelated file to be treated as part of the pack for closeout, because the manifest's source_refs are authoritative and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty. No implementation, validation, or boundary impact.
findings.recommendation: Optionally refine the scope-generation heuristic so a path is placed in exactly one of untracked_implementation_files vs unrelated_dirty_files_preserved, and exclude planning docs like new-session-handoff.md from untracked_implementation_files. No action required for CP00-387 closeout.
